figures.caption: Figure 2. Embodied navigation case study under SPINE framework: (a) Task Definition: long-range planning from orient to destination; (b) Research Hypotheses: visualization of H1 (Semantic Compensation) and H2 (Heuristic Decoupling); (c) Qualitative Analysis of H1 and H2 through SR and SPL metrics.
Figure 3. Experimental settings and results: simulated environment (left), AGV platform (middle), and real-world experiments (right).

Figure 1. Conceptual architecture of SPINE maps the evolution of technical primitives across the four EAI life cycle stages (columns) against the four privacy levels (rows). The vertical gradient illustrates a strategic shift from utility-first environments at L1 (Park) to privacy-critical scenarios at L4 (Bedroom).
Table 1. Multi-criterion privacy classification matrix in SPINE.
